sentence: Never I have seen such a beautiful sunset.
correct: Never have I seen such a beautiful sunset.
explanation: After negative adverbs at the start (<Never>), use inversion: <Never have I seen>.

sentence: It is essential that he comes to the meeting on time.
correct: It is essential that he come to the meeting on time.
explanation: Subjunctive after <essential that>: use base form <come>, not <comes>.

sentence: Hardly she had finished speaking when the audience applauded.
correct: Hardly had she finished speaking when the audience applauded.
explanation: After <Hardly> at the start, use inversion: <Hardly had she finished>.

sentence: The committee recommended that the proposal is accepted.
correct: The committee recommended that the proposal be accepted.
explanation: Subjunctive after <recommended that>: use <be accepted>, not <is accepted>.

sentence: Not only she speaks French, but she also speaks German.
correct: Not only does she speak French, but she also speaks German.
explanation: After <Not only> at the start, use inversion: <Not only does she speak>.

sentence: It is vital that the patient takes the medication regularly.
correct: It is vital that the patient take the medication regularly.
explanation: Subjunctive after <vital that>: use base form <take>, not <takes>.

sentence: Seldom we see such dedication in young employees.
correct: Seldom do we see such dedication in young employees.
explanation: After <Seldom> at the start, use inversion: <Seldom do we see>.

sentence: The doctor insisted that she stays in bed for a week.
correct: The doctor insisted that she stay in bed for a week.
explanation: Subjunctive after <insisted that>: use base form <stay>, not <stays>.

sentence: No sooner he had arrived than the meeting started.
correct: No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.
explanation: After <No sooner> at the start, use inversion: <No sooner had he arrived>.

sentence: It is important that every student submits the assignment on time.
correct: It is important that every student submit the assignment on time.
explanation: Subjunctive after <important that>: use base form <submit>, not <submits>.

sentence: Under no circumstances you should reveal this information.
correct: Under no circumstances should you reveal this information.
explanation: After <Under no circumstances>, use inversion: <should you reveal>.

sentence: She demanded that he apologises immediately.
correct: She demanded that he apologise immediately.
explanation: Subjunctive after <demanded that>: use base form <apologise>, not <apologises>.

sentence: Only after the exam I realised my mistake.
correct: Only after the exam did I realise my mistake.
explanation: After <Only after> at the start, use inversion: <did I realise>.

sentence: The manager suggested that the meeting is postponed.
correct: The manager suggested that the meeting be postponed.
explanation: Subjunctive after <suggested that>: use <be postponed>, not <is postponed>.

sentence: At no time the suspect was seen near the building.
correct: At no time was the suspect seen near the building.
explanation: After <At no time>, use inversion: <was the suspect seen>.

sentence: It is crucial that the government acts quickly.
correct: It is crucial that the government act quickly.
explanation: Subjunctive after <crucial that>: use base form <act>, not <acts>.

sentence: Rarely you find such quality at this price.
correct: Rarely do you find such quality at this price.
explanation: After <Rarely> at the start, use inversion: <Rarely do you find>.

sentence: The board proposed that the CEO resigns.
correct: The board proposed that the CEO resign.
explanation: Subjunctive after <proposed that>: use base form <resign>, not <resigns>.

sentence: Not until midnight the storm finally subsided.
correct: Not until midnight did the storm finally subside.
explanation: After <Not until> at the start, use inversion: <did the storm subside>.

sentence: It is necessary that each participant signs the consent form.
correct: It is necessary that each participant sign the consent form.
explanation: Subjunctive after <necessary that>: use base form <sign>, not <signs>.

sentence: Little they knew about the surprise that awaited them.
correct: Little did they know about the surprise that awaited them.
explanation: After <Little> at the start, use inversion: <Little did they know>.

sentence: She requested that the report is completed by Friday.
correct: She requested that the report be completed by Friday.
explanation: Subjunctive after <requested that>: use <be completed>, not <is completed>.

sentence: In no way this decision should affect your work.
correct: In no way should this decision affect your work.
explanation: After <In no way>, use inversion: <should this decision affect>.

sentence: The law requires that every driver has insurance.
correct: The law requires that every driver have insurance.
explanation: Subjunctive after <requires that>: use base form <have>, not <has>.

sentence: Only when she left I understood how much she meant to me.
correct: Only when she left did I understand how much she meant to me.
explanation: After <Only when> at the start, use inversion: <did I understand>.
